channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Virginia track athlete facing assault charge after baton strike she said was accidental
item.description: Alaila Everett said it was an accident but court records show she is now facing a potential assault and battery charge.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KTLA 5

item.title: EPA likely to move to further limit federal protections for wetlands
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday announced it will reconsider the reach of the nation’s bedrock clean water law and likely further limit the wetlands it covers, building on a Supreme Court decision two years ago that removed federal protections for significant areas.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education Department layoffs gut its civil rights office, leaving discrimination cases in limbo
item.description: The Education Department’s civil rights branch is losing nearly half its staff in the Trump administration’s layoffs, effectively gutting an office that already faced a backlog of thousands of complaints from students and families across the country.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration drops lawsuit against company over alleged abuse at its child migrant shelters
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration moved to drop a civil lawsuit Wednesday against the largest provider of housing for migrant children over allegations of sexual abuse and harassment of unaccompanied minors, saying it also would no longer be using that company's services.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 20 charged with murder after 3 die inside Nevada prison
item.description: Charges against the 20 defendants include first-degree murder and attempted murder with gang enhancements, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: Third suspect in Southern University student's hazing death arrested
item.description: Autopsy results for Caleb Wilson have not yet been released as the coroner's office awaits findings from additional testing.
item.dc:creator: Allison Bruhl, NewsNation

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman catches man driving her stolen car
item.description: A man is now in custody and facing an auto theft charge after a woman allegedly caught him driving her stolen car.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina House Republicans fight with each other over the budget
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — At least three-quarters of South Carolina's House members approved the more than 100 sections of the state's $14 billion spending plan this week, but is wasn't as easy as that makes it seem.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Education secretary: Mass layoffs first step toward total shutdown
item.description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon affirmed late Tuesday that mass layoffs are the first step toward shuttering the Education Department — a long-time goal of President Donald Trump's since his time on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: EU retaliates as Trump steel, aluminum tariffs take effect
item.description: After announcing the response, the EU released a 99-page list of possible items that could be subject to tariffs, including fruit and vegetables, meat, alcoholic beverages, nicotine vapes and chewing gum.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: The US is short nearly 4 million homes
item.description: Builders faced barriers like zoning and supply costs, which make producing affordable homes challenging.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia anti-Israel protest ringleader Mahmoud Khalil faces court hearing on detention
item.description: A Manhattan federal judge will hear a challenge to the detention of Mahmoud Khalil, one of the ringleaders of anti-Israel protests at Columbia University last year who the Trump administration is trying to deport.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hotel horror stories emerge after American college student goes missing as resort insists no connection
item.description: The Dominican Republic resort where University of Pittsburgh student Sudiksha Konanki went missing experienced a lengthy power outage just hours before Virginia resident vanished.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Affordable housing threatened as Trump halts $1 billion slated for extending life of aging buildings
item.description: The Associated Press has obtained a document showing that the Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrat David Gottfried easily wins Minnesota House special election, restoring a 67-67 power split
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Voters in a Minnesota House district at the center of post-election drama over chamber control decisively chose Democrat David Gottfried on Tuesday, tying the House and ending a short-lived Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE, DEA arrest criminal illegal aliens on idyllic New England island
item.description: Two illegal immigrants were arrested in Nantucket, Massachusetts, on Tuesday, by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Drug Enforcement Administration agents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: AG concludes charges in gang-related Nevada prison brawl that left 3 inmates dead
item.description: Twenty people have been charged in connection to a 2024 Nevada prison brawl that ended with three dead inmates, authorities said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why you'll no longer see 'excessive heat warnings' in the forecast
item.description: It may be hard to think about sweltering temperatures in early March but excessive heat watches and warnings are getting a new name.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: University of Pittsburgh student missing in Dominican Republic: What we know
item.description: The search involves joint efforts by authorities in the Dominican Republic, the United States and India.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, WGN 9

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote was not the decisive one, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Police release more details on murder of InfoWars reporter Jamie White as search for suspects continues
item.description: Police in Austin, Texas, believe InfoWars reporter Jamie White was shot and killed when he interrupted suspects possibly burglarizing his car at his apartment complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WGN 9

item.title: What is known about the deadly January air crash between a passenger jet and US Army helicopter
item.description: Federal aviation officials will continue making sure that planes and helicopters no longer share the same airspace over the nation's capital in the wake of January's midair collision, which killed 67 people and was the deadliest U.S. plane crash in more than two decades.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Time is running out for thousands to claim $1 billion in 2021 tax refunds
item.description: Over 1.1 million people have unclaimed federal income tax refunds from 2021, which are in jeopardy of becoming government property if not claimed soon, with a median return of $781 and potential refunds worth up to $6,728 in Earned Income Tax Credit.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas measles outbreak rises to 223 cases
item.description: The measles outbreak in the South Plains region of Texas has grown to 223 cases. 29 of those infected have been hospitalized and one school-aged child has died, according to a new update from the Texas Department of State Health Services.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stark, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: Masked 'Box Demon' spooks homeowner
item.description: Police are searching for a suspicious person who wore a mask and approached a homeowner's door while referring to themselves as the "Box Demon" in York County, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NTSB urges ban on some helicopter flights at Reagan airport after deadly collision
item.description: Federal investigators looking for the cause of the January collision between a passenger jet and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people recommended a ban on some helicopter flights Tuesday to improve safety.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 3 killed after medical helicopter crashes in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died after a medical helicopter crashed near the Natchez Trace in Madison County, Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Howell, Fox 5

item.title: Computer owners duped in antivirus scam to get over $25M: What to know about payments
item.description: More than $25 million in payments will go out this week to customers who were tricked into paying for fraudulent tech support offered by two companies.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Arrest of Palestinian activist stirs questions about protections for students and green card holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The arrest of a Palestinian activist who helped organize campus protests of the war in Gaza has sparked questions about whether foreign students and green card holders are protected against being deported from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Canada slaps electricity tariffs on New York, Minnesota, Michigan
item.description: This surcharge will affect electricity sales for 1.5 million homes and businesses across Michigan, Minnesota and New York,
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Arizona prisoner set to be executed next week passes up chance to ask for reprieve
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A prisoner scheduled to be executed next week in what would be Arizona’s first use of the death penalty in over two years has passed up a chance to ask for a reprieve from his death sentence.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court takes up Colorado conversion therapy case
item.description: The Supreme Court granted a Colorado therapist’s request to review her challenge to a state law banning mental health care providers from engaging minors in conversion therapy, a discredited practice that attempts to change a person’s sexual orientation or gender identity to align with heterosexual
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, ABC 4

item.title: President Trump defends tariff policy
item.description: President Donald Trump continues to defend tariffs as a way to address unfair trade and illegal drugs coming across the border, but critics warn they'll cause higher prices for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, WGN 9
